```python
# test_title_case.py
import pytest

from your_module import title_case      # replace `your_module` with the actual module name


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("input_s", "expected"),
    [
        ("hello world", "Hello World"),                       # all-lowercase, 2 words
        ("hElLo WoRLD", "Hello World"),                       # mixed casing
        ("Hello", "Hello"),                                   # single word
        ("  hello   world  ", "Hello World"),                 # leading / trailing / multiple spaces
        ("hello\tworld\nagain", "Hello World Again"),         # tabs and newlines as whitespace
        ("123abc test-case", "123abc Test-case"),             # non-alphabetic first char and hyphen
        ("mañana ünicode", "Mañana Ünicode"),                 # basic Unicode support
        ("HELLO WORLD " * 1000, ("Hello World " * 1000).strip())  # large input (performance / no crash)
    ],
)
def test_title_case_happy_paths(input_s, expected):
    """
    General ‘happy path’ expectations for a variety of normal inputs.
    """
    assert title_case(input_s) == expected


def test_title_case_empty_string():
    assert title_case("") == ""


def test_title_case_whitespace_only():
    # Function should strip all whitespace and return empty string.
    assert title_case("   \t  \n ") == ""


@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_input", [None, 123, 3.14, ["not", "a", "string"]])
def test_title_case_bad_types_raise_attribute_error(bad_input):
    """
    Passing a non-string should raise AttributeError because the object
    does not implement `.split()`.
    """
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        title_case(bad_input)
```
